query: Could Mercury's ring current produce a measurable magnetic storm like depression?

row.paper: Zhao, J.T., et al., 2022. Observational evidence of ring current in the magnetosphere of Mercury. Nature Communications, 13(1), p.924. DOI:10.1038/s41467-022-28521-3

tool_input.summary: To assess whether Mercury develops a Dst-like magnetic depression from a ring/partial-ring current, you’ll primarily use MESSENGER in-situ magnetic field (MAG) time series, ideally after subtracting an internal field model (KT17 residual products), and compare with energetic ion/plasma pressure proxies from EPPS (FIPS/EPS). Spacecraft geometry and coordinate transforms come from the MESSENGER SPICE kernel set (PDS3 NAIF volume). The catalog shows these as PDS4 bundles for MAG/EPPS and a PDS3 volume for SPICE; I did not find PDS3 MAG/EPPS science datasets in the catalog search results.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Core dataset for detecting storm-like depressions: calibrated vector B-field at multiple cadences/coordinate systems across Mercury orbital phase (2004–2015).

candidates.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides magnetic-field residuals after removing the KT17 internal field model, helping isolate external current systems (ring/partial ring, tail, magnetopause) relevant to Dst-like depressions.

candidates.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Mercury Solar Orbital (MSO) Residual Data
candidates.reasoning: Convenient residuals in MSO coordinates for superposed-epoch analyses versus local time and magnetospheric region (dayside vs nightside).

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Raw Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Useful if you want to redo calibration or inspect high-rate/raw artifacts during large disturbances.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Ion composition/energy distributions and derived plasma moments to estimate plasma pressure enhancements that would support a ring/partial-ring current causing B depressions.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS EPS DDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic particle environment for identifying injections/energization episodes that could intensify a ring/partial-ring current and correlate with MAG depressions.

candidates.title: MESSENGER SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE kernels for spacecraft trajectory, attitude, and time/geometry needed to compute equatorial components (Dst-like proxies) and separate magnetospheric regions when interpreting MAG signatures.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Could Mercury's ring current produce a measurable magnetic storm like depression?

MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle
MESSENGER MAG Raw Data Bundle

Mercury Atmospheric and Surface Composition Spectrometer

Mercury Dual Imaging System Wide Angle Camera

MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Raw Bundle
MESSENGER EPPS Calibrated FIPS Data Bundle
MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
MESSENGER SPICE FILES

dataset.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
dataset.description: This data set consists of the MESSENGER MAG calibrated observations. The MAG experiment is a miniature three-axis ring-core fluxgate magnetometer with low-noise electronics. There are five MAG data products, which mainly differ in the coordinate system. For each type the data are provided at full resolution, and one or more averaging intervals.

dataset.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains the MESSENGER Energetic Particle and Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) calibrated observations, also known as DDRs. The system encompasses 2 instrument subsystems - the Energetic Particle Spectrometer (EPS) and the Fast Imaging Plasma Spectrometer (FIPS).

content.error: Malformed dataset ID: 'MESS-E/V/H-SPICE-6-V1.0'. Expected PDS3 format (e.g., 'MRO-M-HIRISE-3-RDR-V1.1') or PDS4 LIDVID (e.g., 'urn:nasa:pds:cassini_iss::1.0'). Use pds_catalog_search_tool to find valid dataset IDs first.

parts.args: {"summary":"To assess whether Mercury develops a Dst-like magnetic depression from a ring/partial-ring current, you’ll primarily use MESSENGER in-situ magnetic field (MAG) time series, ideally after subtracting an internal field model (KT17 residual products), and compare with energetic ion/plasma pressure proxies from EPPS (FIPS/EPS). Spacecraft geometry and coordinate transforms come from the MESSENGER SPICE kernel set (PDS3 NAIF volume). The catalog shows these as PDS4 bundles for MAG/EPPS and a PDS3 volume for SPICE; I did not find PDS3 MAG/EPPS science datasets in the catalog search results.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-calibrated::1.0","path":null,"title":"MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Core dataset for detecting storm-like depressions: calibrated vector B-field at multiple cadences/coordinate systems across Mercury orbital phase (2004–2015)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-kt17-model-residuals::1.0","path":null,"title":"MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides magnetic-field residuals after removing the KT17 internal field model, helping isolate external current systems (ring/partial ring, tail, magnetopause) relevant to Dst-like depressions."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-kt17-model-residuals:data-sci-mso::1.0","path":null,"title":"MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Mercury Solar Orbital (MSO) Residual Data","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Convenient residuals in MSO coordinates for superposed-epoch analyses versus local time and magnetospheric region (dayside vs nightside)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-raw::1.0","path":null,"title":"MESSENGER MAG Raw Data Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Useful if you want to redo calibration or inspect high-rate/raw artifacts during large disturbances."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-epps-fips-derived::1.2","path":null,"title":"MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Ion composition/energy distributions and derived plasma moments to estimate plasma pressure enhancements that would support a ring/partial-ring current causing B depressions."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-epps-eps-derived::1.1","path":null,"title":"MESSENGER EPPS EPS DDR Bundle","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Energetic particle environment for identifying injections/energization episodes that could intensify a ring/partial-ring current and correlate with MAG depressions."},{"dataset_id":"MESS-E/V/H-SPICE-6-V1.0","path":null,"title":"MESSENGER SPICE FILES","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"naif","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"SPICE kernels for spacecraft trajectory, attitude, and time/geometry needed to compute equatorial components (Dst-like proxies) and separate magnetospheric regions when interpreting MAG signatures."}]}
